$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `provider_models` repo UPSERT (minted-
values / remap) path — the deferred `upsertModel` method. One committed file
drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-provider-models-upsert-fixture.ts + provider-models-upsert-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness provider_models upsert tier-2 test).
`upsertModel(data)` finds an existing row by (provider, modelId, modelType ??
'chat', baseUrl ?? undefined) via `findByProviderAndModelId`; if found it
UPDATES with the FULL data (id + createdAt preserved, updatedAt minted to
now); else it CREATEs (minting id + createdAt + updatedAt all = now).
CRITICAL baseUrl predicate: v4 only constrains baseUrl when it is TRUTHY
(`if (baseUrl) query.baseUrl = baseUrl`). A null/undefined baseUrl leaves the
column UNCONSTRAINED (it does NOT mean 'match a NULL baseUrl'). Op 1 below
upserts a null-baseUrl payload against a null-baseUrl seed row and MUST update
it, not create a duplicate — this is the predicate under test.
This is the MINTED-VALUES form: the upsert ops pin NOTHING (no id, no
timestamps). Both sides mint their own random UUIDs + wall-clock timestamps;
the harness reconciles them with ONE normalization (first-seen id remap +
createdAt/updatedAt placeholder), sorted by the natural key `modelId` (an
input — each final row's modelId is distinct). The createdAt==updatedAt
invariant is DROPPED (an upsert-update mints a fresh updatedAt while keeping
the original createdAt).
Banks: contextWindow/maxOutputTokens both null and integer-valued REAL (the
integer-collapse in the dump), deprecated/experimental both 0/1, baseUrl both
null and non-null, an upsert that UPDATES a seed row (op 1) and an upsert that
UPDATES a freshly-minted-id row (op 3).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one the other
provider_models / folders fixtures use). It is NOT the real Friday pepper and
is safe to commit. SEED rows pin id + timestamps so the starting state is
deterministic.

ops.comment: UPDATE seed gpt-4o via a null-baseUrl upsert: the find constrains only (provider, modelId, modelType) and matches the seed row despite its null baseUrl. Full-data overwrite: new displayName, contextWindow 200000, maxOutputTokens 32768, deprecated=true, experimental=true. updatedAt minted (createdAt preserved -> createdAt != updatedAt).
CREATE: no existing (anthropic, claude-opus-4, chat) row -> mint id + createdAt + updatedAt. Null baseUrl, integer-valued contextWindow/maxOutputTokens, experimental=true.

UPDATE the minted-id row from op 2 (same provider/modelId/modelType, null baseUrl). Full-data overwrite clears contextWindow/maxOutputTokens to NULL and flips both booleans to false. updatedAt minted; createdAt (the op-2 mint) preserved.
CREATE: fresh (openai, text-embedding-3-small, embedding) row with a NON-NULL baseUrl. Integer contextWindow, null maxOutputTokens, deprecated=true.
